summary:
In a standard radical neck dissection including levels I through V, both spinal accessory nerve and submandibular glands may typically be removed or spared based on surgical approach; however, Level IIb lymph nodes are consistently targeted due to their inclusion within these procedures' scope. Agent1 concludes by identifying which specific anatomical feature remains intact despite variations among different types of radical surgeries—namely, the preservation status regarding certain components like the tail of the parotid gland can vary significantly while others such as Lymph Node Levels remain constant across protocols focused solely around complete resections necessary for treating malignancies involving cervical regions. Based primarily upon consistency observed amongst various medical texts detailing comprehensive radical operations targeting malignant growths located near critical vascular pathways requiring extensive tissue clearance without compromising essential functional anatomy unnecessarily beyond required limits dictated purely by oncological necessity alone., thus concluding that Level 2B lymph node retention does occur less frequently compared against other elements listed here making them more reliably absent post-surgery under strict adherence towards classical techniques employed today..
